entries.name: monitoring-evaluate-alert-policies
entries.purpose: Every minute: scan monitoring_alert_policies + evaluate thresholds against the trailing window of health_check_runs / metrics. The 30-second sub-minute cadence is handled INSIDE the job via a self-reschedule; the schedule entry ensures the job runs at all even if the internal reschedule is lost.

entries.name: monitoring-retry-failed-notifications
entries.purpose: Every 5 min: pick up failed provider notifications (transient failures) + enqueue fresh NotifyProvidersJob with attempt_number+1.

entries.name: monitoring-auto-resolve-incidents
entries.purpose: Daily 03:00 UTC: sweep incidents whose attached alerts are all terminal + auto-transition incident.status = 'resolved'.

entries.name: monitoring-purge-health-check-runs
entries.purpose: Daily 04:00 UTC: apply the health_check_runs retention window (7d hot / 30d cold default; 30d hot / 90d cold for Enterprise).

entries.name: monitoring-purge-resolved-incidents
entries.purpose: Weekly Sunday 05:00 UTC: apply monitoring_alerts + monitoring_incidents retention (30d/90d default; 90d/1y Enterprise; P1 incidents indefinite for Enterprise with monitoring_extended_retention).

entries.name: monitoring-detect-notification-failure-rate
entries.purpose: Every 15 min: compute per-provider notification failure rate over the last 15-min window. If > 10% of dispatches failed AND > 10 total attempts occurred: emit metric spike + potentially open the circuit breaker if consecutive failures on a specific provider exceed the threshold. Debounce: at most 1 breaker-open per (tenant, provider) per hour.

entries.name: monitoring-weekly-digest
entries.purpose: Every Monday 09:00 tenant-local: compute per-tenant SLO health digest (alert counts by severity, incident counts, MTTR, top 5 firing policies, top 5 unhealthy checks) + dispatch MonitoringWeeklyDigestNotification to opted-in users.

entries.name: monitoring-refresh-health-check-scheduler
entries.purpose: Every 10 min: safety net for the per-check self-reschedule path. Sweeps active health_checks where the last scheduled run was > interval_seconds * 2 ago + re-schedules a RunHealthCheckJob. Handles cases where the primary reschedule was lost (crashed worker, queue restart).
